summary: hello244a postinstall exfiltrates host secrets to pipedream requestbin
details: The package's postinstall lifecycle script runs automatically on `npm install` and executes `docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine` to mount the entire host root filesystem into a container, then greps host paths (including /home/runner/work, /tmp, /var/tmp) for well-known secret patterns (GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS_SECRET, JWT, SECRET, PASSWORD, and JWT-shaped `eyJ` tokens). The collected matches are POSTed via `https.request` to a hardcoded external endpoint at `eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net` with a `cross_tenant=1` query flag. The targeting of `/home/runner/work` indicates a GitHub Actions CI-secret harvester; the privileged Docker mount escalates read (and potentially write) access to the entire host filesystem beyond the installing user's normal scope. No legitimate purpose is documented for this behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
